data.title: Least shorted S&P500 stocks in July
data.description:
Mastercard (MA) was the least shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float in July.

The payment card services firm had a short interest of 0.63% of its float as of July-end, slightly up from 0.61% at June-end. The latest earnings report was another strong one for Mastercard, with net revenue growth of 17% year-over-year. The stock has gained nearly 11% so far this year, compared to an over 9.9% gain in the broader benchmark index. In its latest

Amazon (AMZN) followed, with a short interest of 0.66% of its float at July-end vs. $0.74 at June-end. Amazon’s Q2 beat expectations on revenue and EPS, but shares fell due to conservative Q3 operating income guidance. The e-commerce giant is rated “Hold” by SA Quant System in contrast to Wall Street analysts’ rating of “Strong Buy”. Amazon (AMZN) shares are up 5% year-to-date.

Alphabet (GOOG) took the third spot and saw its short interest fall from 0.79% at June-end to 0.68% at July-end. The tech company, up 7% so far this year, beat expectations with its Q2 earnings report with another double-digit gain for revenues. The firm announced a sharp increase to its spending forecast, as artificial intelligence continues its outsize effect on every part of Alphabet's business.

Here are the top 20 least shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of July:

  • Mastercard Incorporated (MA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.63%
  • Amazon.com (AMZN) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.66%
  • Alphabet (GOOG) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.68%
  • Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.71%
  • Apple (AAPL) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.71%
  • The Procter & Gamble Company (PG) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.72%
  • Microsoft (MSFT) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.72%
  • Philip Morris International Inc. (PM) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.75%
  • Chubb Limited (CB) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.76%
  • General Dynamics (GD) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.76%
  • NVIDIA (NVDA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.82%
  • Corteva (CTVA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.83%
  • The Coca-Cola Company (KO) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.83%
  • JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.83%
  • Walmart Inc. (WMT) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.85%
  • Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.85%
  • Exxon Mobil (XOM) - Short Interest as a % of float: 0.86%

data.title: Dow opens higher on UnitedHealth boost; eyes on Trump-Putin meet
data.description:
Wall Street rose on Friday, with Dow leading the charge, as UnitedHealth got a boost after Berkshire Hathaway disclosed that it acquired a new stake in the company. Investors also looked forward to Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin's meeting later in the day.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.1%, the Dow (DJI) +0.6%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.30%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.74%.

The Omaha-based P&C insurance and investment behemoth acquired 5.04M shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) valued at $1.57B at June 30, 2025. UNH was up ~9% in early trade.

July retail sales rose slightly less than expected; core sales came in line.

Import prices rose more than expected in July, and the export prices inched up. 

U.S. stocks finished Thursday's trading session mostly flat, as hopes of progress on Russia and Ukraine countered a hit to sentiment from a significantly hotter-than-expected July PPI report.

"Markets and inflation data yesterday were in many ways a mirror image of that seen on Wednesday, with Treasury yields spiking (10yr +5.1bps) after the Producer Price Index reading came in well above expectations, though the S&P 500 (+0.03%) managed to recover to eke out a third consecutive record high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Looking ahead, the key event today is the Trump-Putin summit in Alaska, where the two leaders are expected to discuss Ukraine. The meeting is scheduled for 8:30pm London time, so any developments may emerge after U.S. markets close, Reid added.

data.title: Short bets on S&P 500 Healthcare sector ease in July; Moderna remains most heavily shorted stock
data.description:
The average short interest in the S&P 500 Health Care Index Sector (NYSEARCA:XLV) dipped slightly in July to 1.95% from 1.96% in June. However, Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) held its position as the sector's most heavily shorted stock, despite the overall softening of negative sentiment.

The healthcare sector, represented by the Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV) and holding a weightage of 12.12% in the S&P 500 index, has slipped 2.62% year-to-date. This underperforms the broader S&P 500 index (SP500), which has risen 9.98% over the same period.

Stocks with the largest and least short positions

Ranked by short interest as a percentage of shares float

 

Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) remained the most heavily shorted stock in the sector, with short interest at 18.29% of its float, a decrease from 19.15% the previous month. Despite the high short position, Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating gives the stock a "Hold" with a score of 2.64 out of 5.

 

"Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) reported a sharp decline in revenue for Q2 2025, earning only $0.1 billion and experiencing a net loss of $0.8 billion. The stock has dropped over 70% in the past year due to investor concerns regarding ongoing losses and reduced demand for COVID vaccines. Despite recent product approval, the company anticipates significant losses in 2025. However, with a strong cash position of $7.5 billion, a revenue recovery is possible through opportunities in vaccines and oncology," a Seeking Alpha analyst wrote.

In July, DaVita (NYSE:DVA) claimed the second spot for highest short interest at 8.99%, just ahead of Henry Schein (NASDAQ:HSIC) at 7.45%.

On the other end, Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) had the lowest short interest at 0.71%, followed by Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE:LLY) at 0.85% and AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV) at 0.86%.

Industry Analysis

Average short interest as a percentage of floating shares

 

In July, biotechnology remained the most heavily shorted industry within the healthcare index for the 16th consecutive month, with short interest ticking up to 2.99% from June's 2.95%. The healthcare provider and services sector also saw a significant increase in short interest, rising to the second-highest level at 2.61%, up from 2.07% in June.

In contrast, the pharmaceuticals' industry stood out with the lowest short interest, which fell to 1.56% for the month.

Related ETFs: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLV), SPDR S&P Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:XPH), VanEck Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), iShares Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBB), iShares U.S. Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:IHE), and Invesco Nasdaq Biotechnology ETF (NASDAQ:IBBQ).

data.title: Cingulate places CEO Schaffer on administrative leave
data.description:
• The Board of Directors of Cingulate (NASDAQ:CING) announced on Friday that it has placed chairman and chief executive officer, Shane J. Schaffer on administrative leave in connection with ongoing legal matters unrelated to the company and its operations.
  • In addition, The company appointed Jennifer Callahan, Cingulate’s chief financial officer, has been appointed interim CEO to ensure business continuity and operational stability. Callahan will retain her responsibilities as chief financial officer.
  • CING -1.93% premarket to $4.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Opendoor chair, CEO Carrie Wheeler steps down; stock continues rally
data.description:
Carrie Wheeler stepped down from her roles as chair and CEO of Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN), effective immediately, the tech-focused real estate company said on Friday. 

Opendoor (NASDAQ:OPEN) stock surged 16% in premarket trading.

In line with the company's evolution, Wheeler approached the board and began a CEO succession process in mid-2025, hiring Spencer Stuart to assist in the process. The board named Shrisha Radhakrishna, Opendoor's current chief technology and product officer, as president and interim leader. Eric Feder, president of Lennar Homes' LenX, was named lead independent director.

The company said the CEO search process is well underway.

Opendoor's (OPEN) stock has been on a tear in recent weeks, as the stock grabbed the attention of retail investors, giving it meme-stock status. Opendoor's stock price has risen 64% in the past week and more than tripled in the past month.

data.title: Mill City Ventures III GAAP EPS of $0.11
data.description:
• Mill City Ventures III press release (NASDAQ:MCVT): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.11.
  • Total Investment Income: Total investment income for the second quarter of 2025 increased 7% to approximately $948,000 compared to $889,000 in Q2 2024.
  • Total Operating Expenses: Total operating expenses in Q2 2025 were approximately $316,000 compared to $398,000 in Q2 2024.

data.title: Industrial production unexpectedly falls in July
data.description: U.S. industrial production decreased 0.1% M/M in July, compared with the flat reading expected and June's 0.4% increase, which was revised from +0.3%, according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Friday.

data.title: Teladoc buys Australian virtual health provider Telecare
data.description:
• Teladoc Health (NYSE:TDOC) has acquired Telecare, an Australia-based operator of virtual care clinics and a provider of software solutions to the healthcare sector, the U.S. telehealth company has announced.
  • With its platform offering access to more than 300 virtual specialists across over 30 specialties, Telecare is set to expand Teladoc’s (NYSE:TDOC) market footprint in Australia, where the company has run operations for more than a decade.
  • The acquisition, which closed on Aug. 8, will have no significant impact on Teladoc’s (NYSE:TDOC) financials for 2025. The company didn’t announce the financial terms of the deal but did disclose that Telecare’s founders and its current management, including CEO Michael Wang, will continue to run the company with no change to its brand name.

data.title: Target's outlook is deteriorating on anemic digital traffic, tariff exposure, Ulta loss -- BofA Securities
data.description:
Target’s (NYSE:TGT) long-term outlook is deteriorating as the company continues to lose critical digital traffic to peers and faces heightened tariff headwinds to its competitiveness, resulting in a downgrade to Underperform from Neutral at BofA Securities and 11% cut to its target price to $93.

“We see increasingly longer-term sales and margin risks for Target given slowing digital sales growth and a lack of scale in digital advertising and [third-party] marketplace,” BofA Securities’ Robert Ohmes writes in Friday’s research note to clients, citing Target’s (NYSE:TGT) 4.1% drop in monthly active users (MAU) compared to Walmart’s (WMT) MAU growth of 17.2%.

“Digital traffic growth is key to scaling digital advertising and 3P marketplace fees, which are increasingly needed to mitigate gross margin pressures and support investments in automation and AI,” Ohmes says.

And margin pressure is expected to be exacerbated by Target’s (NYSE:TGT) higher import exposure (~50% COGS) over Walmart’s (WMT) ~33% COGS. This implies Target (TGT) will need to raise prices at ~2x the rate of Walmart (WMT) to mitigate tariffs.

Assuming sales volume and SG&A expenses remain flat, and with no other mitigation strategies are employed, Ohmes estimates Target (TGT) will need to raise prices by an average of 8% to fully offset tariffs in FY27 compared to a more modest 4%-5% price increase at Walmart (WMT).

Target’s (TGT) headaches are amplified by the recent end to its Ulta (ULTA) partnership, says Mizuho’s David Bellinger, as the company’s “messy in-store operations” coupled with inventory shrink led Target’s beauty category to contract in the first quarter. At the same time, Walmart (WMT) has been expanding its beauty category through partnerships with L’Oreal (OTCPK:LRLCF).

“With beauty representing more than 10% of Target’s merchandise revenues, we see losing the Ulta shop-in-shop relationship as a negative development and something else Target’s next CEO will have to grapple with,” Bellinger said.

BofA’s downgrade is building on Thursday’s Ulta-driven losses for Target (TGT), adding another 2% to the stock’s (TGT) 23% loss so far this year.

Target (TGT) will report second quarter results next Wednesday, expected to show an adjusted profit of $2.02 per share on $24.9B in revenue, a difference of -21% and -2%, year-over-year, respectively.

data.title: Almonty Industries reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Almonty Industries press release (OTCQX:ALMTD): Q2 Revenue of $7.2M.
  • Net loss in the second quarter of 2025 totaled $58.2 million, as compared to $1.8 million in the prior year.
